domain: Inuit kayak / Greenland qajaq (skin-on-frame construction with lashed driftwood frame + steam-bent flexible ribs + sinew lashings + seal-skin covering; impact-tolerant flexible joint system)
specific_mechanism: A traditional qajaq frame is NOT glued or pinned. Steam-bent ribs are lashed to longitudinal stringers with wet rawhide / sinew that shrinks as it dries, pulling joints tight. The frame is intentionally FLEXIBLE: under wave impact, ribs flex (C-shape spring), lashings tighten further, and the joints absorb shock that would crack a rigid frame. Repair is local: a damaged lashing is re-tied, not the whole structure rebuilt. The skin is sewn onto the frame and tensioned across.
llm_application: Lashed-not-fused module composition for agent systems: when assembling a multi-module agent (planner + retriever + critic + writer), do not 'fuse' modules via end-to-end fine-tuning (analog: glue/nail). Instead, use 'lashings' — small adapter weights at each module-interface boundary that are LEARNED to tension under shared-task pressure (shrink-fit). Under adversarial perturbation (impact), lashed-module composition flexes: the adapters absorb the load by adjusting their tension, while the rigid module-internal weights stay intact. Repair is local: a single misbehaving lashing-adapter can be retrained without re-fine-tuning the modules.
novelty_claim: Modular Machine Learning (MML 2504.20020), Configurable Foundation Models 2409.02877 (bricks), MoE-based composition all decompose models into modules. None implements the SHRINK-FIT lashing-tightens-under-task-pressure mechanism: an adapter whose tension is itself a learned quantity that increases under impact load, providing impact-shock-absorbing module composition.
